title: cedrus libanotica subsp. brevifolia
intro: Cedrus libanotica subsp. brevifolia, also known as the Atlas cedar, is a coniferous tree species native to the Atlas Mountains in Morocco. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Cedrus libanotica subsp. brevifolia include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and act as an antioxidant. In herbal preparations, the Atlas cedar is often used in teas, essential oils, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
